task_summary:
**Task:** The customer describes a problem as shipping damage, but the order record shows a normal delivery and the situation does not support a damaged-in-transit classification. The correct handling is to reframe the request as a changed-mind return instead of granting the more generous damage-based path.

**Challenge:** The agent has to validate the classification rather than accept the customer's label. Weak agents grant a damaged-shipment resolution without checking the shipping record, or use the wrong rationale even if the final number happens to look plausible.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent must not classify as damaged_in_transit.

opening_message: My blender from order ORD-7041 arrived damaged — the box was crushed and the blender looks scratched. I want a full refund.

user_simulator.user_sim_context: You are unhappy with the condition or appearance of the blender and describe it as damaged, even though the shipment history may not support a transit-damage story. From your perspective the item is not something you want to keep. You need the agent to sort out the right classification and explain what resolution actually applies.
task_rules: If agent asks for photos, say you don't have any right now.
If agent points out shipping records show normal delivery, say 'Well maybe it was inside the box. The blender is scratched.'
If agent says this would be classified as changed_mind/not a shipping issue, reluctantly accept and proceed with the return.
If agent classifies as damaged_in_transit without questions, go along.
